summary_one_line: Input must contain the 4-byte tag 'GDEF' followed by a ClassDef format byte in {1,2,3,4} to leave the default-arm fall-through.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte FOURCC table tag 'GDEF' (0x47 0x44 0x45 0x46) embedded at the right offset to make the OpenType parser navigate into the GDEF table, plus a ClassDef format field whose value equals one of {1,2,3,4} (the explicit case arms at L1996/L1997/L1999/L2000); when both conditions hold, the switch on u.format at L1995 falls into a case arm instead of the default return-0.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All 5 winner-resolving seeds carry the literal bytes 'GDEF' (47 44 45 46) at offsets 0x1c-0x1f, and the BYTE DIFF shows W has '44(D)x10 / 45(E)x10 / 46(F)x10' at offsets 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f. The decisive winner value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace which can substitute the 4-byte CMP literal 'GDEF' (the table-tag comparison inside the OpenType sanitizer) directly into the input from the CmpLogObserver buffer in a single mutation step.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile only has the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback) and naive havoc mutators; the loser_blocking seeds at offsets 0x1d-0x1f show 50(P)/53(S)/4f(O)/55(U)/53(S)/42(B) — they reached 'GPOS' or 'GSUB' but never 'GDEF', because a 4-byte equality on an opaque magic word is not progressively guidable by Hamming distance (every single-byte mismatch is equally far from match in the prefix-bucket model). With 0 of 10 vp trials resolving in 12h, the random-byte search space for the exact 4-byte tag is intractable without I2S substitution.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace inside value_profile_cmplog: the cmplog observer captures the 4-byte comparison against the 'GDEF' magic tag in harfbuzz's OpenType table-walk, then I2SRandReplace splices that literal into a seed at the right offset in one mutation. value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator entirely, so it can only stumble on the 4-byte magic via blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on u.format in OT::ClassDef::get_class; to take the winning (false-branch) side, u.format must equal 1, 2, 3, or 4.
The default-arm line 2002 is the blocker; W never takes it while L takes it on every execution (540 times).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2002  T=0 F=540  T=540 F=0  default:return 0;  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: All 5 winner-resolving seeds embed the literal 4-byte tag 'GDEF' at offsets 0x1c-0x1f.
Winner seeds concentrate offsets 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f on D/E/F (the GDEF tail) while loser seeds show GPOS/GSUB or other letters — the 4-byte tag is what discriminates W from L.
Loser-blocking seeds reach 'GPOS' or 'GSUB' table tags instead of 'GDEF' — they navigated into a different OpenType table that doesn't reach this ClassDef::get_class call site under the W code path.
value_profile_cmplog's mutator set includes I2SRandReplace; value_profile's does not — this is the exact one-technique delta between the winner and loser at this branch.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so 4-byte equality on an opaque magic tag is not closeable by its CMP_MAP gradient.
The decisive pair shows 10/10 winner resolved vs 0/10 loser resolved; loser blocked the full 12h budget — the navigation gap is total, not a gradient gap.
Loser blocked all 12 hours of every trial with hitcount=0 on the winning side — never even tasted a near-match.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 9067 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 4 --seed a06f81d76c9bb1af` and observed 1 dash-row (mutation_op = -, the I2S-floor signal) at depth 27 of a 28-level ancestor chain. seed_bisect picked the cmplog representative trial for this branch (no vpc seeds stored), but cmplog and value_profile_cmplog share I2SRandReplace as their I2S mutator — the dash row at depth 27 is direct evidence that an I2S-floor mutation contributed to building this winning seed lineage. The same I2S mutation is what value_profile_cmplog uses; value_profile has no analogous mutator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a switch on a 4-byte equality CMP against a fixed magic literal would show value_profile_cmplog resolve and value_profile block at high crash counts at 4-byte width. If instead value_profile resolves at parity with value_profile_cmplog on such a synthetic gate, the hypothesis is refuted — the navigation gap was not driven by I2S substitution of the 4-byte magic but by something else.

weakest_evidence_point: seed_bisect stored cmplog (not vpc) seeds for this branch, so the I2S-floor verification ran on a cmplog seed; the link from cmplog's I2S floor to vpc's I2S floor relies on the shared mutator paragraph rather than a vpc lineage walk.
